meaning: n.可移动的东西adj.可移动的,活动的
examples: What do we know about the book A Moveable Feast?
There are three books I reread annually. The first, which I take to reading every spring is Emest Hemningway's A Moveable Feast.
In college, when I created my online dating (约会) profile, in the "favorite books" section I put One Hundred Years of Solitude, A Moveable Feast, White Fang and so on.
Many puppeteers also carve the puppets, which can have between twelve and twenty-four moveable joints.
The scheme would see existing weirs at Knostrop and Crown Point removed and replaced with moveable weirs.
